# Fix: job/invoice/proforma numbers generated by "read all, find max, +1"

## What was actually happening

Job numbers, invoice numbers, agency invoice numbers, and proforma numbers
were all generated the same way: fetch a batch of existing records, parse
the highest number out of them client-side, add 1, and save that back as
the "next number" counter. Two people (or two tabs/devices) doing this at
the same moment can both read the same "current highest," and both save
the same "next" number — a real, if rare, race condition on numbers that
end up on real invoices sent to clients.

There's also two pre-existing separate bugs this migration fixes as a byproduct:

- Settings → Invoicing has an **"Agency Starting Number"** field
  (`S.agencyInvStart`, defaults to 2001) that is saved correctly but was
  never actually read anywhere — agency (`AGN-`) invoices silently shared
  the exact same counter as regular (`INV-`) invoices instead of having
  their own series.
- **Bigger one, found while fixing this**: creating a proforma/quote
  (`createProforma()`) sends a `type: 'proforma'` field to the `invoices`
  table — but that table has no `type` column at all. Every attempt to
  create a proforma has been failing outright with a Postgres error
  ("column type does not exist"), shown to you as a red "Failed: ..."
  toast. This is presumably why `invoices` currently has zero rows in your
  live database. This migration adds the missing column so the app code
  (already correct) finally works.

## The fix

Same pattern already used successfully for the portal's `CR-` request
numbers: replace client-side counting with atomic Postgres sequences, one
per number series, plus small `SECURITY DEFINER` RPC functions the app
calls to get the next value. A sequence's `nextval()` is atomic by
construction — no read, no race condition, no dependency on RLS or on
every client agreeing on what "the highest number so far" is.

```sql
-- Fixes the "column type does not exist" error — invoices never had this
-- column even though the app has always written to it for proformas.
ALTER TABLE invoices ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS type text;

-- ── Seed each sequence from whatever the highest real number already in
--    your data is, so it continues on cleanly instead of restarting at 1
--    and colliding with existing records. (Your invoices table is
--    currently empty, so these will all just seed from their defaults —
--    that's expected and fine.) ─────────────────────────────────────────

-- Regular job numbers (e.g. JOB-0001) — excludes CR- jobs, which have
-- their own series below. (This block already ran successfully last time
-- — CREATE SEQUENCE IF NOT EXISTS makes it safe to run again.)
DO $$
DECLARE maxn int;
BEGIN
  SELECT COALESCE(MAX((regexp_match(jobnum, '(\d+)$'))[1]::int), 1000) INTO maxn
  FROM jobs WHERE jobnum !~ '^CR';
  IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_sequences WHERE schemaname='public' AND sequencename='job_num_seq') THEN
    EXECUTE format('CREATE SEQUENCE job_num_seq START %s', maxn + 1);
  END IF;
END $$;

-- CR- prefixed jobs (portal-originated), 3-digit pad, separate series.
-- (Also already ran successfully last time — safe to run again.)
DO $$
DECLARE maxn int;
BEGIN
  SELECT COALESCE(MAX((regexp_match(jobnum, '^CR(\d+)$'))[1]::int), 0) INTO maxn
  FROM jobs WHERE jobnum ~ '^CR\d+$';
  IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_sequences WHERE schemaname='public' AND sequencename='job_cr_seq') THEN
    EXECUTE format('CREATE SEQUENCE job_cr_seq START %s', maxn + 1);
  END IF;
END $$;

-- Regular (INV-) invoices — excludes proforma and agency, which have their
-- own series. Filters by the number's own prefix rather than the type/
-- invoicetype columns, since that's simpler and matches what the app
-- itself already does when scanning for the next number.
DO $$
DECLARE maxn int;
BEGIN
  SELECT COALESCE(MAX((regexp_match(number, '(\d+)$'))[1]::int), 1000) INTO maxn
  FROM invoices WHERE number NOT LIKE 'PF-%' AND number NOT LIKE 'AGN-%';
  IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_sequences WHERE schemaname='public' AND sequencename='inv_num_seq') THEN
    EXECUTE format('CREATE SEQUENCE inv_num_seq START %s', maxn + 1);
  END IF;
END $$;

-- Agency (AGN-) invoices — now genuinely separate from regular invoices,
-- fixing the dead "Agency Starting Number" setting.
DO $$
DECLARE maxn int;
BEGIN
  SELECT COALESCE(MAX((regexp_match(number, '(\d+)$'))[1]::int), 2000) INTO maxn
  FROM invoices WHERE number LIKE 'AGN-%';
  IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_sequences WHERE schemaname='public' AND sequencename='agn_num_seq') THEN
    EXECUTE format('CREATE SEQUENCE agn_num_seq START %s', maxn + 1);
  END IF;
END $$;

-- Proforma (PF-) quotes.
DO $$
DECLARE maxn int;
BEGIN
  SELECT COALESCE(MAX((regexp_match(number, '(\d+)$'))[1]::int), 0) INTO maxn
  FROM invoices WHERE number LIKE 'PF-%';
  IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_sequences WHERE schemaname='public' AND sequencename='proforma_num_seq') THEN
    EXECUTE format('CREATE SEQUENCE proforma_num_seq START %s', maxn + 1);
  END IF;
END $$;

-- ── RPCs the app calls to atomically get the next number in each series ──

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION next_job_num() RETURNS int
LANGUAGE sql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public AS $$
  SELECT nextval('job_num_seq')::int;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION next_cr_num() RETURNS int
LANGUAGE sql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public AS $$
  SELECT nextval('job_cr_seq')::int;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION next_inv_num() RETURNS int
LANGUAGE sql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public AS $$
  SELECT nextval('inv_num_seq')::int;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION next_agn_num() RETURNS int
LANGUAGE sql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public AS $$
  SELECT nextval('agn_num_seq')::int;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION next_proforma_num() RETURNS int
LANGUAGE sql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public AS $$
  SELECT nextval('proforma_num_seq')::int;
$$;

-- ── Admin override: lets Settings → Invoicing's "Starting Number" fields
--    actually fast-forward a sequence (e.g. if you want the next invoice
--    to jump to 5000). Restricted to a fixed allow-list of sequence names
--    so it can't be used to run arbitrary SQL. ─────────────────────────

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION admin_set_seq(seq_name text, new_start int) RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public AS $$
BEGIN
  IF seq_name NOT IN ('job_num_seq','job_cr_seq','inv_num_seq','agn_num_seq','proforma_num_seq') THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid sequence name: %', seq_name;
  END IF;
  EXECUTE format('ALTER SEQUENCE %I RESTART WITH %s', seq_name, GREATEST(new_start, 1));
END;
$$;

REVOKE EXECUTE ON FUNCTION next_job_num() FROM PUBLIC;
REVOKE EXECUTE ON FUNCTION next_cr_num() FROM PUBLIC;
REVOKE EXECUTE ON FUNCTION next_inv_num() FROM PUBLIC;
REVOKE EXECUTE ON FUNCTION next_agn_num() FROM PUBLIC;
REVOKE EXECUTE ON FUNCTION next_proforma_num() FROM PUBLIC;
REVOKE EXECUTE ON FUNCTION admin_set_seq(text,int) FROM PUBLIC;

GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION next_job_num() TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION next_cr_num() TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION next_inv_num() TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION next_agn_num() TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION next_proforma_num() TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION admin_set_seq(text,int) TO authenticated;
```

## What changes for you day to day

Nothing visible — same prefixes (`JOB-`, `CR`, `INV-`, `AGN-`, `PF-`), same
padding, same Settings fields. The only functional differences:

- Two people completing jobs / creating invoices at the exact same moment
  can no longer end up with the same number.
- Agency invoices now genuinely have their own numbering series starting
  from "Agency Starting Number" instead of secretly sharing the regular
  invoice counter.
- The app code has a fallback: if this SQL hasn't been run yet (or the RPC
  call fails for any reason), it silently falls back to the old scan-based
  method, so nothing breaks if this migration is delayed.

## Note on the "Job Starting Number"

There's currently no Settings UI field to view/override the *job* number
counter (only invoices have one). The job sequence is seeded correctly
from your existing data either way — it just isn't admin-editable yet. Say
the word if you want that control added; it's a small follow-up.
